headline: DISCOVERY SILVER Q1 2026: 60,269 oz Gold at $4,908 Realized Price — Porcupine on Full-Year Track
snippet: Discovery Silver Corp. produced 60,269 oz of gold at Porcupine in Q1 2026, processing 700,000 tonnes at 2.96 g/t with 90.6% recovery. The company realized $4,908/oz on gold sold and remains on track for full-year guidance of 260,000–300,000 oz, with throughput set to increase as the Hollinger open pit ramps up.

headline: NEWMONT REPORTS Q1 2026 After Bell Today — Analysts Forecast $2.07 EPS, $6.77B Revenue
snippet: The world's largest gold miner reports Q1 2026 results after the close today. Consensus expects $2.07 EPS — up 65.6% year-over-year — and $6.77B in revenue. Newmont has beaten estimates in each of the last four quarters; watch for AISC guidance and production volumes at gold near $4,750.

headline: GOLD SLIDES TO $4,700 as Second-Round Iran Ceasefire Talks Collapse, Dollar Firms
snippet: Gold fell toward $4,700/oz on April 23 as the planned second round of US-Iran peace negotiations fell apart, reversing a brief recovery to $4,752 the prior session. Junior gold miners tracked the metal lower as the Middle East conflict continues to inject intraday volatility.

headline: GoldSeek: GOLD BOUNCED BACK as Dip Buyers Absorb Selling — GDX Breaks Multi-Month Resistance
snippet: GoldSeek analysts note structured dip-buying absorbing gold's recent selloff, with the GDX senior miner ETF breaking above the $42–$46 resistance zone that had capped it since the conflict-driven decline. Junior miners (GDXJ) lag but are beginning to show improving relative strength.

headline: China Gold Imports UP to Start 2026 — Investment Demand Soars Even as Jewellery Stalls
snippet: Chinese gold imports rose in the first two months of 2026 as investment demand surges to multi-year highs, with gold-backed funds adding 46 tonnes through February. Jewellery is struggling at elevated prices, but bar-and-coin buying and ETF inflows are more than compensating — an important demand floor for the gold price.

headline: GOLD CANDLE Acquires Fokus Mining for $63M, Raises $65M to Build Quebec Abitibi Platform
snippet: Gold Candle Ltd. closed its acquisition of Fokus Mining Corp. at $0.26/share (equity value ~$63M) and simultaneously completed a $65M private placement, leaving the combined entity with ~230M shares outstanding. Fokus's flagship Galloway Project, 20 km west of Rouyn-Noranda on the Cadillac-Larder Lake Fault, hosts 1.42 Moz Au inferred.

headline: GLOBAL LITHIUM ASX:GL1 Locks In $75M Offtake Prepayment From Jiangsu Lopal for Manna Project
snippet: Global Lithium Resources (ASX: GL1) signed a binding deal with Jiangsu Lopal Tech: a US$75M offtake prepayment facility plus A$7.32M equity placement for 5% of the company, securing 40% of annual production from the Manna Lithium Project for 10 years at a floor price of US$1,000/t for the first three years. FID targeted for end-2026.

headline: GALWAY METALS Intersects 6.1 g/t Gold Over 19m Near-Surface at New Brunswick's Clarence Stream
snippet: Galway Metals (TSXV: GWM) returned 6.1 g/t Au over 19.0m, including 18.3 g/t over 3.0m, at the South Deposit of Clarence Stream in New Brunswick with four rigs active. The intercept sits within 50 vertical metres of surface inside the existing 2022 resource pit shell; an updated resource estimate is underway on the 922,000 indicated / 1.33 Moz inferred deposit.

headline: MP Materials: Wedbush Initiates at $90 — 'The Only Real Play' on U.S. Rare Earth Independence
snippet: Wedbush initiated MP Materials (NYSE: MP) at Outperform with a $90 price target, arguing the Mountain Pass operator is the only investable vehicle for domestic U.S. rare earth independence. The company doubled NdPr oxide output in 2025 to a record 2,599 tonnes — critical feedstock for EV and defence magnets that China currently supplies at roughly 90% of global refining.

headline: MAGNA MINING Q4 2025: First Positive Cash Margin at McCreedy West Cu-Ni-PGM Mine in Sudbury
snippet: Magna Mining (TSXV: NICU) achieved a positive cash margin of $3.3M at McCreedy West in Sudbury's Copper Cliff South mine complex in Q4 2025 — the first quarter of positive operating cash flow since acquisition. The result signals improving unit economics at the copper-nickel-PGM operation as metal prices remain elevated.
